title: Roasted Algerian White Yam Tagine
description: A hearty and flavorful North African dish featuring roasted white yam and bonito flakes, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This kosher-friendly recipe is easy to make and serves as a great meal prep option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the white yam cubes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the yam cubes on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic, ground cumin, and smoked paprika to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the bonito flakes and cook for an additional 1 minute.
To serve, divide the roasted white yam cubes among individual plates and top with the onion and bonito flake mixture.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
